title: Object 80
wikitext:
__NOTOC__ __NOEDITSECTION__ {{Transparent Theme 2}} {{Background|file=Sprig Farm.jpg}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 80: "'''Holiday Baubles'''"}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Bauble.png|caption=Picture of an Holiday Bauble in an unknown level. The bauble, seems to be just about fully charged. Taken by M.E.G}} Object 80, also known as "'''Holiday Baubles'''", are round glass ornaments about the size of an orange, each containing a small pine sprig and visible "snow." The interior surface is covered with tiny clear beads resembling crushed ice, and they have metal caps with hanging loops. Only the orange size has been recorded. The glass seems harder than normal glass but remains breakable. The bauble emits waves of cool air in a small radius of up to 5 cm. These baubles are very rare in the Backrooms. It is unknown how they arrived here. They may randomly manifest within the Backrooms. No records exist showing how or where they manifest, so their origin remains a mystery. It is theorized they appear in areas where no wanderers are present, similar to [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. The main detail about these baubles is that they can send a person to one of several different Backrooms levels. After many tests by the M.E.G., it was concluded they transport wanderers to different Christmas-based levels. Several attempts to break the baubles were reported to the Kauer Research Group. Breaking a bauble causes the snow inside to melt immediately and releases a mixture of gases and a liquid form of [[Object 31|Burkharts Gas]]. Because the gas is exposed to warmer temperatures, it condenses into liquid. Other gases released when the bauble breaks produce the cooling effects, but these gases could not be captured, so the exact mechanism is unknown, though it is believed to result from constant chemical reactions produced by mixing gases inside the bauble. If a holiday bauble suddenly produces more snow than usual and begins to glow, it is charged and ready to use. A wanderer may store it for later use or rip out the silver hanging loop. Doing so, will cause the bauble to glow so brightly, that it will temporarily blind nearby wanderers, however, this blinding has never been recorded to cause injury or harm, to wanderers. After the brief blinding, the glow rapidly dims and the wanderer, will finds themselves in a Christmas-based level, such as [[Level 941]]. After sending someone to a level, the bauble will be completely empty, howevfer, the pine sprig will still remain inside the bauble. After a while, snow will slowly beginto fall from the top, in the empty bauble, gradually refilling and "charging" the bauble. When it is about one-third full, it will glow again and be ready. Usually it takes a week to refill, but the charging process takes longer if ambient temperatures are below 10°C. Each degree below 10°C delays charging by one day. The bauble is hard to find and is typically located in dark corners or inconvenient spots. They very rarely they appear in obvious places. The floor is usually frosted around them due to the cooling waves they produce. These cooling waves reach -5°C, making prolonged contact dangerous. It is advised to keep a bauble in a backpack rather than in pockets or held in hand. ==Usage== Due to its traveling ability and rarity, it is extremely valuable to many groups and sells for high prices. The bauble may be used to leave dangerous levels or escape life‑threatening situations. The pine sprig obtained by breaking the bauble can be made into a healing herbal tea that cures many diseases, colds, allergies, and inflammations. The pine sprig can be used only five times, making it very expensive. It can be planted (by just sticking it into soil), but it will only grow at temperatures of −30°C or below. There are only two pine‑sprig farms, owned by wealthy businessmen. {{Blurimage|file=Sprig Farm.jpg|caption=Picture of a sprig farm on an unkown level, taken by Grondo Roosenboom.}} The bauble, will bring one to the following Levels: *[[Level 941]] *[[Level 125]] *[[Level 224]] *[[Level 9.01]] "Scary Baubles", will bring one, to the following Level(s): *[[Level 9.31]] ==Variants== ===Object 80: "Christmas baubles"=== This is known, to be the most common variant to find, though still rare. This bauble will bring one to Christmas-based levels. Rarely this variant may bring one to a new year-based level. ===Object 80-A:”Scary Baubles”=== This sub-variant of the bauble is extremely rare. Only thirty have ever been found, thus beeing sold for very high prices. It behaves like the normal variant but contains a pumpkin instead of a pine sprig, has an orange hue, and holds an unknown purple substance instead of "snow." Cooking the pumpkin is not as safe as drinking the tea from a pine sprig, as doing so causes a wanderer to fall asleep for 24 hours, then waking with chest pain and extreme exhaustion. Ten of the thirty owners reported sleep paralysis and severe nightmares. This variant sends users to Halloween‑themed levels rather than Christmas levels and emits milder cold waves. ===Object 80-B "Heavenly Bauble"=== This variant is the rarest and presumably the most valuable object in the Backrooms. It looks exactly like the original variant, except that there is nothing inside it but a white, foggy substance that swirls around in the bauble. Only three of these have ever been found. The first bauble ever found belongs to the M.E.G., the second to the Kauer Research Group, and the last to an anonymous wanderer. None of the owners have dared to break this variant, as it is far too valuable to do so and therefore what happens if it is broken is unknown. This bauble most commonly sends wanderers to Class-habitable levels, rarely to Class 0 levels, and in very rare cases the M.E.G. has recorded that the bauble can send someone to Class 1 levels. Unlike the other variants, this bauble emits waves of warm temperatures and sometimes vibrates for unknown reasons. {{Blurimage|file=BaubleFootageMeg745232.mp4|caption=A footage of the bulb, beeing used.}} ==History== 24 December 1990: Christiane Müller, a veteran Backrooms explorer, found a strange glass bauble on Level 1 and allowed Backrooms Today to document, study, and write an article about it. Since then, the search for these baubles began. Backrooms Today also stated that anyone who handed a bauble over to them would receive a large amount of supplies. 30 October 2000: An explorer who wished to remain anonymous found one of the first “Scary Baubles.” He handed the object to the Kauer Research Group in exchange for shelter and supplies. The Kauer asked Backrooms Today to publish an article and announced they were starting a research project on the object. When the news spread, the value of the original variant fell, but it remained expensive. 6 June 2003: The M.E.G. was exploring a very dangerous level when they discovered a bauble containing a foggy, swirling substance. They took it to their base for further inspection and realized it was similar to the original Object 80 variant. After pulling out the silver hanging loop, they later found themselves in a safe level. The M.E.G. reported this to Backrooms Today, which published an article titled “The Bauble Returns.” 12 December 2022: The Kauer Research Organization found another Object 80‑B, which by that time had become one of the most valuable items in the Backrooms. 2 December 2024: An anonymous wanderer found another Object 80‑B but requested to remain private.
'''Open credits'''
==Content: Timothy223. *Thanks for reading, '''{{USERNAME}}'''. {{User:Timothy223/TimothyLevels}} ===Happy Holidays!===
